# Per-host process-diversity baseline with tiered classification (T1059)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Builds a per-host execution profile to baseline normal activity during case-scoping: total executions, distinct binaries, and distinct command lines per host. A nested `if()` collapses distinct-binary count into low/medium/high diversity tiers, helping an analyst tell a focused workstation from a noisy build server or a host running attacker tooling with high command-line churn. Counting distinct command lines (not just binaries) catches living-off-the-land abuse where one binary is driven with many different argument strings.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and agent_hostname != null
| alter bin_lc = lowercase(actor_process_image_name)
| comp count() as total_execs, count_distinct(bin_lc) as distinct_bins, count_distinct(actor_process_command_line) as distinct_cmdlines by agent_hostname
| alter diversity = if(distinct_bins > 10, "high", if(distinct_bins > 3, "medium", "low"))
| sort desc total_execs
| limit 30
```
